Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method reduces storage requirements by over 95% while simultaneously yielding significant performance gains across numerous benchmarks and base models. ColParse thus bridges the critical gap between the fine-grained accuracy of multi-vector retrieval and the practical demands of large-scale deployment, offering a new path towards efficient and interpretable multimodal information systems.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Сжатие векторных представлений документов через структурное понимание макета
ru.desc: ColParse представляет инновационный подход к поиску в визуальных документах, используя модель распознавания структуры для генерации компактных векторных представлений фрагментов документов. Метод учитывает информацию о макете страницы и объединяет локальные эмбеддинги подизображений с глобальным векторным представлением всей страницы. Предложенный алгоритм достигает сокращения требуемой памяти более чем на 95% без потери качества поиска, что решает проблему масштабируемости в системах многовекторного поиска. Экспериментальная оценка демонстрирует улучшение производительности на множестве бенчмарков, что делает метод практически применимым для больших систем мультимодального поиска информации.

en.title: Efficient Document Retrieval with Layout-Aware Embeddings
en.desc: ColParse is a new method for visual document retrieval that focuses on understanding the layout of documents, not just the text. It creates smaller, layout-informed sub-image embeddings that help in forming a compact representation of the document. This approach significantly reduces storage needs by over 95% while still improving retrieval performance. By combining these embeddings with a global vector, ColParse effectively addresses the challenges of multi-vector architectures in large-scale applications.

zh.title: ColParse：高效的视觉文档检索新方法
zh.desc: ColParse是一种文档解析方法，通过生成布局信息的子图像嵌入，创建紧凑且结构化的表示，以用于视觉文档检索。该方法在保持性能的同时，实现了超过95%的存储减少，解决了现有多向量架构在存储方面的瓶颈。ColParse结合了局部的子图像嵌入和全局页面向量，形成了一种新的多向量表示。通过大量实验，ColParse在多个基准测试中显示出显著的性能提升，推动了多模态信息系统的高效和可解释性。

papers.title: Operator Learning Using Weak Supervision from Walk-on-Spheres
papers.abstract: Abstract Neural PDE solvers trained with Monte Carlo-based weak supervision achieve improved accuracy, faster training, and reduced memory usage compared to traditional physics-informed methods.  					AI-generated summary Training neural PDE solvers is often bottlenecked by expensive data generation or unstable physics-informed neural network (PINN) involving challenging optimization landscapes due to higher-order derivatives. To tackle this issue, we propose an alternative approach using Monte Carlo approaches to estimate the solution to the PDE as a stochastic process for weak supervision during training. Leveraging the Walk-on-Spheres method, we introduce a learning scheme called Walk-on-Spheres Neural Operator (WoS-NO) which uses weak supervision from WoS to train any given neural operator. We propose to amortize the cost of Monte Carlo walks across the distribution of PDE instances using stochastic representations from the WoS algorithm to generate cheap, noisy, estimates of the PDE solution during training. This is formulated into a data-free physics-informed objective where a neural operator is trained to regress against these weak supervisions, allowing the operator to learn a generalized solution map for an entire family of PDEs. This strategy does not require expensive pre-computed datasets, avoids computing higher-order derivatives for loss functions that are memory-intensive and unstable, and demonstrates zero-shot generalization to novel PDE parameters and domains. Experiments show that for the same number of training steps, our method exhibits up to 8.75times improvement in L_2-error compared to standard physics-informed training schemes, up to 6.31times improvement in training speed, and reductions of up to 2.97times in GPU memory consumption. We present the code at https://github.com/neuraloperator/WoS-NO

ru.title: Прогулка по сферам: дешёвая супервизия для нейронных решателей уравнений
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый метод обучения нейронных операторов для решения дифференциальных уравнений в частных производных, основанный на слабой супервизии через методы Монте-Карло. Авторы используют алгоритм Walk-on-Spheres для генерации дешёвых и шумных оценок решений, избегая вычисления дорогостоящих производных высоких порядков. Предложенный подход не требует предварительно вычисленных датасетов и демонстрирует нулевую обобщающую способность на новые параметры и области определения. Эксперименты показывают значительное улучшение точности (до 8.75 раза), скорости обучения (до 6.31 раза) и снижение потребления памяти GPU (до 2.97 раза) по сравнению с традиционными физико-информированными методами.

en.title: Revolutionizing Neural PDE Solvers with Monte Carlo Weak Supervision
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method for training neural PDE solvers using Monte Carlo-based weak supervision, which enhances accuracy and efficiency. The proposed Walk-on-Spheres Neural Operator (WoS-NO) leverages stochastic processes to provide weak supervision, allowing for effective training without the need for expensive datasets. By avoiding the computation of higher-order derivatives, the method reduces memory usage and stabilizes the optimization process. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in error rates, training speed, and memory consumption compared to traditional physics-informed neural networks.

zh.title: 利用弱监督提升神经PDE求解器的效率与准确性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的神经偏微分方程（PDE）求解器训练方法，利用基于蒙特卡洛的弱监督来提高准确性和训练速度，同时减少内存使用。通过Walk-on-Spheres方法，我们引入了一种称为Walk-on-Spheres神经算子（WoS-NO）的学习方案，利用弱监督来训练神经算子。该方法通过随机表示来降低蒙特卡洛步行的成本，生成便宜且噪声较大的PDE解的估计。实验结果表明，与传统的物理信息训练方案相比，我们的方法在L_2误差、训练速度和GPU内存消耗上均有显著改善。

papers.title: Making Reconstruction FID Predictive of Diffusion Generation FID
papers.abstract: Abstract A new metric called interpolated FID is proposed that shows strong correlation with generation FID in diffusion models, addressing the poor correlation issue between reconstruction FID and generation FID.  					AI-generated summary It is well known that the reconstruction FID (rFID) of a VAE is poorly correlated with the generation FID (gFID) of a latent diffusion model. We propose interpolated FID (iFID), a simple variant of rFID that exhibits a strong correlation with gFID. Specifically, for each element in the dataset, we retrieve its nearest neighbor (NN) in the latent space and interpolate their latent representations. We then decode the interpolated latent and compute the FID between the decoded samples and the original dataset. Additionally, we refine the claim that rFID correlates poorly with gFID, by showing that rFID correlates with sample quality in the diffusion refinement phase, whereas iFID correlates with sample quality in the diffusion navigation phase. Furthermore, we provide an explanation for why iFID correlates well with gFID, and why reconstruction metrics are negatively correlated with gFID, by connecting to results in the diffusion generalization and hallucination. Empirically, iFID is the first metric to demonstrate a strong correlation with diffusion gFID, achieving Pearson linear and Spearman rank correlations approximately 0.85. The source code is provided in https://github.com/tongdaxu/Making-rFID-Predictive-of-Diffusion-gFID.

ru.title: От реконструкции к поколению: метрика интерполяции для диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новая метрика под названием interpolated FID (iFID), которая показывает сильную корреляцию с FID поколения в диффузионных моделях. Авторы решают проблему плохой корреляции между FID реконструкции и FID поколения, используя интерполяцию латентных представлений соседних примеров из набора данных. Показано, что FID реконструкции коррелирует с качеством образцов на этапе уточнения, тогда как iFID коррелирует с качеством на этапе навигации диффузии. Впервые предложена метрика, достигающая корреляции Пирсона и ранговой корреляции Спирмена около 0.85 с generation FID диффузионных моделей.

en.title: Interpolated FID: Bridging the Gap Between Reconstruction and Generation Metrics
en.desc: This paper introduces a new metric called interpolated FID (iFID) that effectively measures the quality of generated images in diffusion models. It addresses the issue of poor correlation between reconstruction FID (rFID) and generation FID (gFID) by using nearest neighbor interpolation in the latent space. The authors demonstrate that iFID correlates strongly with gFID, achieving high Pearson and Spearman correlations, while rFID is shown to correlate poorly with gFID. The findings provide insights into the relationship between reconstruction metrics and sample quality during different phases of diffusion processes.

zh.title: 插值FID：提升扩散模型生成质量的关键指标
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的度量标准，称为插值FID（iFID），它与扩散模型中的生成FID（gFID）具有很强的相关性，解决了重建FID（rFID）与生成FID之间的相关性差的问题。iFID是rFID的一个简单变体，通过在潜在空间中检索每个数据集元素的最近邻并插值其潜在表示来计算。研究表明，rFID与扩散精炼阶段的样本质量相关，而iFID与扩散导航阶段的样本质量相关。iFID是第一个展示与扩散gFID强相关性的度量，相关系数达到约0.85。

papers.title: Layer by layer, module by module: Choose both for optimal OOD probing of ViT
papers.abstract: Abstract Intermediate layers in vision transformers provide better representations than final layers, with performance degradation due to distribution shifts between pretraining and downstream data, and optimal probing strategies depend on the magnitude of these shifts.  					AI-generated summary Recent studies have observed that intermediate layers of foundation models often yield more discriminative representations than the final layer. While initially attributed to autoregressive pretraining, this phenomenon has also been identified in models trained via supervised and discriminative self-supervised objectives. In this paper, we conduct a comprehensive study to analyze the behavior of intermediate layers in pretrained vision transformers. Through extensive linear probing experiments across a diverse set of image classification benchmarks, we find that distribution shift between pretraining and downstream data is the primary cause of performance degradation in deeper layers. Furthermore, we perform a fine-grained analysis at the module level. Our findings reveal that standard probing of transformer block outputs is suboptimal; instead, probing the activation within the feedforward network yields the best performance under significant distribution shift, whereas the normalized output of the multi-head self-attention module is optimal when the shift is weak.

ru.title: Оптимальные представления скрыты внутри слоёв трансформера
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется поведение промежуточных слоёв предобученных vision transformers. Авторы показывают, что промежуточные слои часто дают более дискриминативные представления, чем финальный слой, и основной причиной деградации производительности является сдвиг распределения между данными предобучения и целевой задачей. Через линейный проbing на различных бенчмарках классификации изображений они выявили, что оптимальная стратегия зондирования зависит от величины этого сдвига распределения. При сильном сдвиге лучше всего использовать активации внутри feedforward сети, а при слабом сдвиге — нормализованный выход multi-head attention модуля.

en.title: Unlocking the Power of Intermediate Layers in Vision Transformers
en.desc: This paper investigates how intermediate layers in vision transformers provide superior representations compared to the final layer, particularly in the context of distribution shifts between pretraining and downstream tasks. The authors conduct linear probing experiments to demonstrate that these shifts significantly impact the performance of deeper layers. They find that probing the activations within the feedforward network is most effective under strong distribution shifts, while the output of the multi-head self-attention module performs best when shifts are minimal. This research highlights the importance of selecting appropriate probing strategies based on the nature of the data distribution shift.

zh.title: 中间层的力量：提升视觉变换器性能的关键
zh.desc: 本论文研究了视觉变换器中间层的表现，发现它们通常比最终层提供更好的特征表示。研究表明，预训练和下游数据之间的分布变化是导致深层性能下降的主要原因。通过对多个图像分类基准的线性探测实验，我们分析了不同模块的表现，发现标准的探测方法并不理想。我们提出在显著分布变化时，探测前馈网络的激活可以获得最佳性能，而在变化较小的情况下，多头自注意力模块的归一化输出效果最佳。

papers.title: nabla-Reasoner: LLM Reasoning via Test-Time Gradient Descent in Latent Space
papers.abstract: Abstract Gradient-based optimization integrated into LLM decoding enables efficient reasoning enhancement with reduced model calls.  					AI-generated summary Scaling inference-time compute for Large Language Models (LLMs) has unlocked unprecedented reasoning capabilities. However, existing inference-time scaling methods typically rely on inefficient and suboptimal discrete search algorithms or trial-and-error prompting to improve the online policy. In this paper, we propose nabla-Reasoner, an iterative generation framework that integrates differentiable optimization over token logits into the decoding loop to refine the policy on the fly. Our core component, Differentiable Textual Optimization (DTO), leverages gradient signals from both the LLM's likelihood and a reward model to refine textual representations. nabla-Reasoner further incorporates rejection sampling and acceleration design to robustify and speed up decoding. Theoretically, we show that performing inference-time gradient descent in the sample space to maximize reward is dual to aligning an LLM policy via KL-regularized reinforcement learning. Empirically, nabla-Reasoner achieves over 20% accuracy improvement on a challenging mathematical reasoning benchmark, while reducing number of model calls by approximately 10-40% compared to strong baselines. Overall, our work introduces a paradigm shift from zeroth-order search to first-order optimization at test time, offering a cost-effective path to amplify LLM reasoning.
affiliations: The University of Texas at Austin

ru.title: От поиска к оптимизации: градиентный подход для повышения рассуждений LLM
ru.desc: В статье предлагается nabla-Reasoner — инновационная система, которая интегрирует градиентную оптимизацию в процесс генерации текста LLM для улучшения логического мышления. Ключевой компонент Differentiable Textual Optimization использует градиенты от модели правдоподобия и функции вознаграждения для итеративного уточнения представлений текста. Авторы доказывают теоретическую эквивалентность этого подхода обучению с подкреплением, регуляризованному расхождением Кульбака-Лейблера. Эксперименты показывают значительное улучшение точности на задачах математического рассуждения при одновременном сокращении количества обращений к модели на 10-40%.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Reasoning with Gradient-Based Optimization
en.desc: This paper introduces nabla-Reasoner, a new framework that enhances reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs) by integrating gradient-based optimization into the decoding process. It utilizes Differentiable Textual Optimization (DTO) to refine the model's output in real-time by leveraging gradient signals from both the model's likelihood and a reward model. The approach not only improves accuracy on reasoning tasks by over 20% but also reduces the number of model calls needed, making it more efficient. Overall, nabla-Reasoner represents a shift from traditional search methods to a more effective first-order optimization strategy during inference.

zh.title: 推理能力的革命性提升
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为nabla-Reasoner的框架，通过将可微优化集成到大型语言模型（LLM）的解码过程中，提升推理能力。核心组件可微文本优化（DTO）利用梯度信号来优化文本表示，从而在推理时动态调整策略。该方法结合了拒绝采样和加速设计，提高了解码的鲁棒性和速度。实验结果表明，nabla-Reasoner在数学推理基准测试中实现了超过20%的准确率提升，同时减少了10-40%的模型调用次数。

papers.title: Physics Informed Viscous Value Representations
papers.abstract: Abstract Physics-informed regularization based on Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation with viscosity solution improves value estimation in offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning through Monte Carlo estimation.  					AI-generated summary Offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning (GCRL) learns goal-conditioned policies from static pre-collected datasets. However, accurate value estimation remains a challenge due to the limited coverage of the state-action space. Recent physics-informed approaches have sought to address this by imposing physical and geometric constraints on the value function through regularization defined over first-order partial differential equations (PDEs), such as the Eikonal equation. However, these formulations can often be ill-posed in complex, high-dimensional environments. In this work, we propose a physics-informed regularization derived from the viscosity solution of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation. By providing a physics-based inductive bias, our approach grounds the learning process in optimal control theory, explicitly regularizing and bounding updates during value iterations. Furthermore, we leverage the Feynman-Kac theorem to recast the PDE solution as an expectation, enabling a tractable Monte Carlo estimation of the objective that avoids numerical instability in higher-order gradients. Experiments demonstrate that our method improves geometric consistency, making it broadly applicable to navigation and high-dimensional, complex manipulation tasks. Open-source codes are available at https://github.com/HrishikeshVish/phys-fk-value-GCRL.
affiliations: College of Engineering, Purdue University, USA
Department of Computer Science, Purdue University, USA

ru.title: Физика оптимального управления для стабильной оценки ценности в офлайн обучении
ru.desc: В статье предлагается физически обоснованная регуляризация для оффлайн обучения с подкреплением на основе уравнения Гамильтона-Якоби-Беллмана. Авторы используют вязкостное решение этого уравнения для улучшения оценки функции ценности в условиях ограниченного охвата пространства состояний и действий. С помощью теоремы Фейнмана-Каца задача переформулируется как математическое ожидание, что позволяет применить метод Монте-Карло для стабильной оценки целевой функции без численной неустойчивости. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что подход улучшает геометрическую согласованность оценок ценности и применим к задачам навигации и сложным манипуляционным задачам в высоких размерностях.

en.title: Enhancing Value Estimation in GCRL with Physics-Informed Regularization
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method for improving value estimation in offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning (GCRL) by using a physics-informed regularization based on the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation. The authors address the challenge of limited state-action space coverage by applying physical constraints to the value function, which helps in maintaining geometric consistency. By utilizing the viscosity solution of the HJB equation, the approach provides a solid theoretical foundation that enhances the learning process through optimal control principles. The method also employs the Feynman-Kac theorem to facilitate Monte Carlo estimation, ensuring stability in complex environments.

zh.title: 物理信息正则化提升强化学习价值估计
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于哈密顿-雅可比-贝尔曼方程的物理信息正则化方法，以改善离线目标条件强化学习中的价值估计。该方法通过在值函数上施加物理和几何约束，利用粘性解来增强学习过程的稳定性。我们的方法结合了最优控制理论，明确地对价值迭代过程中的更新进行正则化和界定。实验结果表明，该方法在导航和高维复杂操作任务中具有更好的几何一致性，具有广泛的应用潜力。

papers.title: Demystifying Action Space Design for Robotic Manipulation Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract Large-scale empirical study demonstrates that action space design significantly impacts robotic policy learning, with delta action prediction improving performance and joint-space/task-space representations offering distinct advantages for control stability and generalization.  					AI-generated summary The specification of the action space plays a pivotal role in imitation-based robotic manipulation policy learning, fundamentally shaping the optimization landscape of policy learning. While recent advances have focused heavily on scaling training data and model capacity, the choice of action space remains guided by ad-hoc heuristics or legacy designs, leading to an ambiguous understanding of robotic policy design philosophies. To address this ambiguity, we conducted a large-scale and systematic empirical study, confirming that the action space does have significant and complex impacts on robotic policy learning. We dissect the action design space along temporal and spatial axes, facilitating a structured analysis of how these choices govern both policy learnability and control stability. Based on 13,000+ real-world rollouts on a bimanual robot and evaluation on 500+ trained models over four scenarios, we examine the trade-offs between absolute vs. delta representations, and joint-space vs. task-space parameterizations. Our large-scale results suggest that properly designing the policy to predict delta actions consistently improves performance, while joint-space and task-space representations offer complementary strengths, favoring control stability and generalization, respectively.
affiliations: Institute for AI Industry Research (AIR), Tsinghua University

ru.title: Проектирование пространства действий: ключ к эффективному обучению робототехнических политик
ru.desc: В этой работе проводится масштабное эмпирическое исследование влияния пространства действий на обучение политик робота в задачах манипуляции на основе имитационного обучения. Авторы систематически анализируют выбор представления действий по двум осям: временной (абсолютные vs. дельта-действия) и пространственной (суставное vs. декартово пространство). На основе более 13000 реальных экспериментов на двуруком роботе и оценки 500+ обученных моделей показано, что предсказание дельта-действий стабильно улучшает производительность. Исследование демонстрирует, что суставное пространство обеспечивает большую стабильность управления, а декартово пространство улучшает обобщение на новые задачи.

en.title: Optimizing Action Space for Enhanced Robotic Learning
en.desc: This paper explores how the design of the action space affects the learning of robotic policies in manipulation tasks. It highlights that predicting delta actions, which represent changes rather than absolute positions, can enhance performance in policy learning. The study also distinguishes between joint-space and task-space representations, showing that each has unique benefits for stability and generalization in control tasks. Through extensive experiments with a bimanual robot, the authors provide insights into optimizing action space design for better robotic performance.

zh.title: 优化动作空间设计，提升机器人学习性能
zh.desc: 本研究表明，动作空间的设计对机器人策略学习有显著影响。通过对13,000多个真实世界的实验进行分析，我们发现预测增量动作可以持续提高性能。我们还比较了关节空间和任务空间的表示，发现它们在控制稳定性和泛化能力上各有优势。整体而言，合理的动作空间设计能够优化机器人学习的效果。
